name: Augment Context Engine
description: Augment Code's proprietary large-codebase context/retrieval engine

developer-org.comment: Augment (Augment Computing, Inc.), a San Francisco AI-coding startup founded in 2022; emerged from stealth April 2024 with a $227M Series B.

implementation-language.comment: Closed, proprietary engine with a custom inference/embedding stack; the implementation language is not publicly disclosed. The distributed Auggie CLI that fronts it is shipped as an npm package, but the engine core's stack is undisclosed.

first-release.comment: First public unveiling of the Context Engine accompanied Augment's April 2024 stealth exit (the 'real-time index for your codebase' blog); general availability of the Augment Agent for VS Code followed in November 2024. Month reflects first announcement. Checked 2026-07-09.

status.comment: Actively sold and developed; the Context Engine is the core of Augment's product line and now also ships as a standalone MCP.

license.comment: The Context Engine is a proprietary, closed-source component of Augment's commercial product.

open-source.comment: Engine core is proprietary. The public github.com/augmentcode/auggie repo is a distribution/docs/examples repo for the CLI (binary shipped via npm), not the engine source.

repository.comment: No public source repository for the engine. The github.com/augmentcode/auggie repo distributes the CLI (npm binary) and hosts docs/examples; the Context Engine itself is closed.

github-stars.comment: Engine is closed-source with no representative repo. The auggie CLI distribution repo had ~249 stars on 2026-07-09, but it is the client, not the engine.

form-factor.comment: Originally bundled inside the Augment Agent (VS Code / JetBrains extension). As of 2026 the engine also ships as a standalone Context Engine MCP — either via the local Auggie CLI (stdio MCP server) or an Augment-hosted HTTP endpoint. Checked 2026-07-09.

standalone-usable.comment: Now usable outside Augment's own agent: 'Plug our Context Engine into Claude Code, Cursor, Zed, and any MCP-compatible agent.' This supersedes the older bundled-only positioning (RESEARCH.md's example still lists Augment as standalone:false). Checked 2026-07-09.

bundled-host.value: Augment Agent (VS Code / JetBrains extension); also Auggie CLI
bundled-host.comment: The engine's original home is Augment's own agent/IDE extensions; it is no longer exclusively bundled since the Context Engine MCP release.

install-mechanism.comment: Auggie CLI (local MCP server) installs via npm (@augmentcode/auggie); the Augment Agent installs as a VS Code / JetBrains editor extension; the remote Context Engine is an Augment-hosted HTTP service.

retrieval-technique.comment: Semantic retrieval over custom-trained embedding/context models with (quantized) vector search; Augment explicitly builds proprietary embeddings rather than generic OpenAI/Pinecone stacks. Marketing describes mapping relationships across files, but the documented core mechanism is embedding-based semantic search. Checked 2026-07-09.

query-interface.comment: Queried semantically with natural-language task context; exposed to agents as MCP retrieval tool calls.

returns.comment: Retrieves the relevant slice(s) of code ('the right slice before the model spends tokens exploring') to feed as context, rather than whole files or a repo blob.

structural-awareness.comment: Augment describes semantic mapping of relationships between files but does not publicly document tree-sitter/LSP/AST-based structural parsing. Internals undisclosed for this closed engine — left null rather than guessing.

chunking-strategy.comment: How code is split before embedding is not disclosed for this closed engine.

reranking.comment: Augment describes ranking/curating and compressing candidates (e.g. '4,456 sources -> 682 relevant'), which implies a selection stage, but a distinct second-stage rerank mechanism is not documented. Left null.

index-location.comment: Augment-hosted remote index on Google Cloud (BigTable/PubSub) for repos connected via the GitHub App; the local Auggie CLI can instead index the working directory locally as a stdio MCP server. Both paths exist. Checked 2026-07-09.

incremental-updates.comment: Maintains a real-time, per-developer index that updates within seconds of a file change rather than rebuilding from scratch.

real-time-watch.comment: Local Auggie CLI 'indexes in real-time as you edit — no manual sync required'; the hosted index auto-syncs when commits are pushed to the default branch.

vector-store.comment: Custom in-house vector search (quantized) backed by Google Cloud infrastructure (BigTable); Augment explicitly avoids third-party vector stores like Pinecone.

embedding-model.value: Custom-trained proprietary Augment context/embedding models (not publicly named)
embedding-model.comment: Augment trains its own context models rather than calling generic embedding APIs; the specific model is not disclosed. Checked 2026-07-09.

mcp-support.comment: Shipped Context Engine MCP — available as a local Auggie CLI stdio MCP server or an Augment-hosted HTTP MCP endpoint.

agent-compatibility.comment: Documented MCP integrations include Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, GitHub Copilot, Zed, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Kilo Code, Kiro, Roo Code, AntiGravity and Droid — i.e. any MCP-compatible agent. Checked 2026-07-09.

ide-integrations.comment: Augment Agent surfaces in VS Code and JetBrains IDEs; the Auggie CLI brings the engine to the terminal.

language-agnostic.comment: Embedding/semantic indexing operates over the whole codebase with no documented parser-support restriction; positioned for large polyglot codebases. Language list not officially enumerated — treated as broadly multi-language. Checked 2026-07-09.

supported-languages.value: Not officially enumerated; positioned as broadly multi-language (embedding-based indexing across the whole repo)
supported-languages.comment: Augment does not publish a supported-language list; the engine is marketed for large polyglot codebases.

code-leaves-machine.comment: In the default/hosted configuration, code is sent to Augment's Google Cloud servers to be embedded and indexed (Augment states it self-hosts inference on GCP and uses Proof-of-Possession to guard file access). The local Auggie CLI indexes on-machine, but retrieval/model inference remains Augment-hosted, so code/queries still egress. Checked 2026-07-09.

local-only-option.comment: No fully offline path: even the local Auggie indexer depends on Augment's hosted embedding/inference stack and account. There is no documented local-embedding-model + local-only mode.

self-hostable.comment: The engine runs on Augment's own Google Cloud infrastructure (SaaS); no on-prem/self-managed deployment of the indexer is offered. Enterprise adds CMEK and compliance controls but not self-hosting. Checked 2026-07-09.

telemetry.comment: As a hosted SaaS, usage data is collected for billing/service; Augment states it does not train AI on your code by default and offers SOC 2 Type II. Treated as opt-out. Checked 2026-07-09.

max-repo-scale.comment: Explicitly engineered for very large repos — Augment cites 100M+ line codebases and indexing/mapping relationships across hundreds of thousands of files.

pricing-model.comment: The Context Engine has no standalone price — it is included in Augment's paid plans (bundled), which are token/credit-based SaaS. Standalone MCP use is billed via token-based usage (list price + 40% service fee).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest published paid plan on 2026-07-09 is Business at $100/mo flat (up to 50 seats, $100 of usage included). The earlier $20/mo Indie and $60/mo Standard individual plans were retired in mid-2026. Time-sensitive; checked 2026-07-09.

maturity.comment: Production-grade engine developed over ~2.5 years, well-funded ($227M), GA since late 2024, deployed on large enterprise codebases — very mature. Slightly below 5 because the standalone Context Engine MCP form is comparatively new/evolving.

active-maintenance.comment: Actively shipping — recent product releases (e.g. Context Engine MCP, Code Review agent Dec 2025) and ongoing CLI updates well within 90 days of the research date.

community-size.comment: Well-funded with notable enterprise adoption, but a smaller developer community than Cursor/Copilot-scale tools (auggie CLI repo ~249 stars). Judged medium.
